tavily.answer: Recent developments in Afghanistan highlight escalating tensions between Afghanistan and Pakistan. According to Afghan officials, Pakistani airstrikes have killed at least 36 civilians and injured over 160 others, while Pakistan claims to have killed 32 militants in the same operations. The conflict has seen multiple airstrikes and ground operations, with Afghanistan condemning the strikes as acts of aggression and brutality. The US State Department has expressed support for Pakistan's right to defend itself against terrorist attacks, while Afghanistan has also launched air strikes into Pakistani territory, further raising cross-border tensions.
